source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
law plays in it, the CSCS is an interesting example in its potential to re-shape
the regulation of corporate action and with it the status quo of international
economic inﬂuence and the content of the political project.
   Cutler argues that law is a function which ‘produces discourses of truth
by mediating conﬂicting claims to power, to knowledge, and to right’,64
thus emphasising the contest of ideas and interests in law. This function
results in the political project of transnational law which embodies ‘the
common sense of transnational capitalist legality’.65 With this theoretical
conception in mind, this chapter describes some features of the CSCS (as
implemented and as envisioned) in order to understand the impact on
corporations as well as its potential to change in the ‘common sense’ of
corporate regulation.
